Details:
Chemical addition of nano-scale zero valent iron (ZVI) for the degradation of molinate can be effective (30 to 92 percent removal with three hours of contact time depending on dose). ZVI-mediated degradation of contaminants typically occurs under fully anoxic conditions; however, for molinate, two studies utilizing stirred batch systems with 100 micrograms per liter molinate found that oxic conditions are needed (no molinate removal was observed under anoxic conditions) [1796, 1797]. Additionally, the rate of molinate degradation increased with decreasing pH.
Chemical addition of aluminum sulphate (alone or in combination with ozone and powdered activated carbon) was considered in one study [723]. By itself, coagulation with aluminum sulfate at 10 to 40 milligrams per liter as Al removed only 15 percent of molinate (551 nanogram per liter initial concentration), compared to 75 and 95 percent removal when in combination with ozone or both ozone and powdered activated carbon.
